title: Secular, cyclical or war-dependent: A ratio to tell you what kind of growth you are buying. 6 stocks with upside potential of up to 29%
summary: Some valuation ratios are used because they are easy. The PE ratio is one of them. It is simple, familiar, and convenient. Not only for the buyer but also for the market participant who is selling that narrative. But markets are rarely simple. A PE ratio tells an investor what the market is paying for a company’s earnings. It does not tell you whether those earnings are growing fast enough, whether growth is durable, or whether the current earnings are close to a cyclical peak. That is why, in many cases, PE is only the first question. It is not the answer.
